Alright, JB's in the house for your daily motivation slash skills training. Let me just tell you one thing here, right? If you are having trouble writing your vision statement out, if you're like, you can't get off the mark again, I'm not a really good writer. You know, that's not how I, it's simply a lack of commitment to your own success. Let me just give you some tough love here. You are smart enough. You possess the sufficient writing skills. There is nothing that's stopping you right now from after hearing this, this video, you go right out and right out your vision statement. Or if you wrote out a crappy one that doesn't really move you, go back and put some more layers into it, put some oomph into it. Remember the key here, the key to writing a great vision is to not allow yourself to be limited by the skill sets and the relationships that you have today, that you possess today. In other words, you can't say, well, how could I think about having this big company, this amazing life when I don't really know everything there is to know about that. I'm not a world-class closer yet. You're very close to becoming one now. If you're on certification, you're closer than you think because you're about these three modules of scripting and then there's looping, there's a bunch of other modules as well. But those two are these outer world killer, Bam Bam 1, 2 punch in, customers flat, how you upsell, cross, you'll get referrals. You'll never be the same again in terms of your skills. But that goes not just with a straight line with everything. You learn as you go. You grow yourself every single day. The mistake people make, the mistake you might be making right now is you write out your vision statement without giving yourself the benefit of the doubt that you will know whatever you need to know. You'll be aligned with the groups and organizations you need to be aligned with. You'll have the people in your life you need to have in your life. Write out your vision statement thinking that way. Think big, think bold, think bright. That's story for this. James Cameron has in my mind, and I'm not a big Avengers fan, but in my mind, he still has the number one and now the number three top grossing movies of all time. I don't consider Endgame to be the number one because they cheated. They did. And I'm a huge Marvel fan, right? But that wasn't right to re-release it just to get a few extra bucks. And I think you probably agree with me. And I know that James Cameron is so awesome that when he releases his next big movie, he'll do it again because that's James Cameron. But when they asked James Cameron, and I really do love Marvel, right? So I don't mean to slight them, but I didn't like that. Anyway, point being, when James Cameron was asked, how is it possible that you have the top two grossing movies of all time? One director. There's so many talented directors out there, right? And this is back when he had one and two. Titanic. I think it was Titanic and Avatar, right? He goes, you know, he goes, when I am thinking about my vision, what I want this to be, I think so ridiculously big that even if I'm half right, I'm still doing great. Get it? He thinks so big. He makes it so bold that even if he doesn't get there, if he just gets halfway there, he's out from everybody else by a country mile. That's how you have to write your vision statement. Now of course, James Cameron has done the work. He possesses the skill sets one would need to possess to achieve that level of success. Guess what? You're doing the work too. You're on this video right now, right? You're hopefully in certification. So you get in the training you need and you deserve. You're growing yourself. You're on that path. Make sure you don't sell yourself short with a half-assed vision statement. All right? So with that, go back to it right now. Think big.
